steps.title: Identify the relationship between the two clauses
steps.content: The first clause says actors showed suppressed brain responses when their names were called during performances. The second clause says those same actors showed normal responses in nonacting contexts. The two findings are opposite — the blank must signal that contrast.

steps.title: Test each choice against the contrast relationship
steps.content:
The transition must indicate that the second finding directly opposes the first.
  • Choice A — conversely,: signals direct opposition between two things — suppressed responses during acting vs. normal responses outside it. ✓
  • Choice B — specifically,: signals that what follows narrows or details what came before, but normal nonacting responses don't narrow the acting finding — they contradict it. ✗
  • Choice C — likewise,: signals similarity, but the two responses are opposite, not the same. ✗
  • Choice D — thus,: signals a logical consequence, but suppressed acting responses do not cause normal nonacting responses. ✗
That is the correct answer.
